revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飛鳥坐神社]]
{{distinguish|{{ill|Asuka Shrine|ja|阿須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|qq=}}|{{ill|Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|ja|添御県坐神社 (奈良市三碓町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sō no Miagata Niimasu Shrine|qq=}}|qq=}} which is the real Asuka Shrine, this was under the name erroneously[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:56, 26 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} despite being under that title [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:31, 18 February 2025 (UTC)|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Asukaniimasu-jinja04n3900.jpg|deity={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q11294873|qid=Q11294873|1=Kayanarumi no Kami|2=ja|3=カヤナルミ}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|prefecture={{ill|Asuka|qid=Q28693136|1=Asuka}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.47969444444445|N|135.8227222222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Nihongo|'''Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine'''|飛鳥坐神社|Asukaniimasu-jinja||qq=}} is a Shinto in the historical capital of Asuka, in Japan. It symbolizes the religious importance of the region during the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} (538 - 710).<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|url=https://www.amazon.ca/Studies-Shinto-Shrines-R-Ponsonby-Fane/dp/1138983225|title=Studies In Shinto & Shrines|last=Ponsonby-Fane|first=R. A. B.|date=2016-05-11|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-138-98322-9|edition=1st edition|language=English|qq=}}</ref> It is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise Shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|fr|Sanctuaires Moto-Ise|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} and its coverage in Ponsbury Fane's book may be useful for helping getting general coverage on the concept. == Historical Context == Before {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} became Japans capital [[Asuka, Yamato|Asuka]] held that honor. Despite the belief that Nara was Japans permanent capital Asuka actually served as the political hub for a longer duration. Unlike a city Asuka was a cluster of settlements where rulers erected structures like palaces and government offices based on their preferences.<ref name=":0" /> Emperor Ingyo ruled from Tootsu Asuka Palace after ascending to power in {{ill|412|en|412|qid=Q32767|lt=412|1=412}} AD. It wasn't until {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suikos|1=Empress Suiko}} reign in {{ill|592|en|592|qid=Q32774|lt=592|1=592}} AD that Asuka officially became the capital holding onto this status until {{ill|719|en|719|qid=Q23537|lt=719 AD|1=719}} with some interruptions. During this era several palaces were constructed, such as Toyoura no Miya, Okamoto no Miya and Kiyomihara no Miya showcasing the impermanent nature of ceremonial buildings, in Asuka.<ref name=":0" /> == Etymology == Historical documents, like the [[Kojiki]] and [[Nihon Shoki]] suggest that the name Asuka is linked to the word "tomorrow" (asu) and terms related to proximity and distance like "Chikatsu" (and "Tohotsu" (. The writing of Asuka characters was influenced by Emperor Temmus era, who favored symbols such as " bird" (Tobutori) for palace names adding to the linguistic and cultural history of the region.<ref name=":0" /> == Shrine Deities == Asuka ni Masu Jinja is prominently mentioned in the [[Engishiki]], a compilation of Shinto rituals from the period (794 1185 AD). The shrine is known for housing four deities with Kamotsumiyae Kotoshironushi no Kami considered as the deity. Kotoshironushi no Mikoto, a son of Okuninushi no Mikoto played a role in stories about granting land in central reed plains to Amaterasus descendants.<ref name=":0" /> The identities of the three deities at Asuka ni Masu Jinja are not entirely clear. However one theory suggests that Kayanarumi no Mikoto might have been one of them residing at Kamunabi Asuka as a deity, for the emperor.<ref name=":0" /> {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|シタテルヒメ|simple|User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|en:draft|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}}, a deity mentioned alongside [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] highlights the bonds present, in the shrines pantheon.<ref name=":0" /> ==Gallery== <gallery mode="packed"> File:Asuka 20190622154603 (48308570132).jpg|alt=|A picturesque view of the Asuka ni Masu Jinja grounds. File:Asuka 20190622154732 (48308456406).jpg|alt=|Another angle of the Asuka ni Masu Jinja grounds showcasing its serene environment. File:Sacred Stone and Shimenawa at Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Sacred Stone and Shimenawa File:Asukaniimasu-jinja, Asuka-Yamaguchi-jinja.jpg|alt=|'''Asuka-Yamaguchi-jinja a subshrine of Asuka Shrine''' File:Chozuya at Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Chozuya at Asuka Shrine File:Two buildings at Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Two buildings at Asuka Shrine File:Asukaniimasu-jinja05n3900.jpg|alt=|A detailed view of the intricate woodwork on one of the shrine buildings. File:Asukaniimasu-jinja06n4592.jpg|alt=|Trees above the shrine File:Many Sessha of Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Many Sessha of Asuka Shrine File:Asukaniimasu-jinja08n3900.jpg|alt=|The main worship area within Asuka ni Masu Jinja. File:Stone with Shimenawa at Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Stone with Shimenawa at Asuka Shrine File:Stones with Shimenawa at Asuka Shrine.jpg|alt=|Stones with Shimenawa at Asuka Shrine File:Asuka 20190622154506 (48308458581).jpg|alt=|Haiden File:Asukaniimasu-jinja, haiden.jpg|alt=|The Haiden (worship hall) of Asuka ni Masu Jinja, an important area for rituals and offerings. File:Asukaniimasu-jinja04n3900.jpg|alt=|A view of the shrine's Haiden, surrounded by natural beauty. File:Asukanimasu shrine.jpg|alt=|A close-up of the Haiden within Asuka ni Masu Jinja. File:Springtime at Asuka ni Masu Jinja.jpg|alt=|Springtime at Asuka ni Masu Jinja, with cherry blossoms enhancing the spiritual ambiance. File:飛鳥坐神社 - panoramio - z tanuki.jpg|alt=|A view of the shrine during the autumn season, showcasing the vibrant foliage. File:Asuka 20190622154151 (48308459676).jpg|alt=|A different angle of the stone pathways within Asuka ni Masu Jinja. File:Asukaniimasu-jinja Torii.jpg|alt=|The grand Torii gate at Asuka ni Masu Jinja, marking the sacred boundary of the shrine. File:Asukaniimasu-jinja, torii.jpg|alt=|A detailed view of the intricate carvings on the Torii at Asuka ni Masu Jinja. File:Asukaniimasu-jinja01n3900.jpg|alt=|A pathway leading up to the shrine, flanked by trees. File:明日香村飛鳥 飛鳥坐神社 Asukaniimasu-jinja, Asuka 2011.4.17 - panoramio.jpg|alt=|A historic view of the shrine captured in 2011. File:明日香村飛鳥 飛鳥坐神社の鳥居 2012.4.12 - panoramio.jpg|alt=|Another view of a Torii at Asuka ni Masu Jinja, framed by lush greenery. File:飛鳥坐神社 - panoramio.jpg|alt=|Road to shrine entrance </gallery> == References == {{commons cat|Asukaniimasu-jinja }}<references />
{{translated page|ja|飛鳥坐神社|version=100351483|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{everybodywiki link|Asuka Shrine}}
[[ja:飛鳥坐神社]]
<!--Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Asukaniimasu-jinja}}
== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine (Q11665959)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Takaichi District, Nara|en|Takaichi District, Nara|ja|高市郡|ar|تاكايشي|ca|Districte de Takaichi|ceb|Takaichi-gun|es|Distrito de Takaichi|fa|منطقه تاکاایچی، نارا|fr|District de Takaichi|id|Distrik Takaichi, Nara|it|Distretto di Takaichi|ko|다카이치군|nl|Takaichi (district)|pl|Powiat Takaichi|ru|Такаити|zh|高市郡|lt=Takaichi district|qid=Q1117901|1=Takaichi District, Nara}} || {{ill|Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine|lt=Asuka-ni-imasu Shrine|ja|飛鳥坐神社|commons|Category:Asukaniimasu-jinja|qid=Q11665959|1=Asuka-ni-Imasu Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings|qid=Q135009157|lt=Ainame|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings}}{{efn|gets offerings for the {{ill|Ainame Festival|ja|相嘗祭|zh|相嘗祭|qid=Q11581944|1=Ainame Festival}} and the lower ranked {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|simple|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|ja|新嘗祭|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}.}}) || {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.479677|135.822745|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180817 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11665959}} [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:明日香村の建築物]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]]
